data.reasoningText: The user wants to make their code testable by replacing `DateTime.UtcNow` with a mockable abstraction. They're on .NET 10, which means `TimeProvider` (introduced in .NET 8) is available as the built-in abstraction. Let me look at their code first.

data.content: Now I can see your `ReportGenerator` class. Since you're on .NET 10, the right abstraction is the built-in `TimeProvider` (available since .NET 8) — no custom wrapper needed.
data.reasoningText:
The user is on .NET 10 and wants to mock time in tests. Since .NET 8+, the built-in `TimeProvider` abstraction is the right approach. I should:

1. Inject `TimeProvider` into the `ReportGenerator` class via constructor injection
2. Replace `DateTime.UtcNow` with `timeProvider.GetUtcNow()`

This is the modern, idiomatic approach for .NET 8+. The `TimeProvider` class is in the `System` namespace and has a `FakeTimeProvider` available in `Microsoft.Extensions.TimeProvider.Testing` for test purposes.


I'll use the testability-wrappers skill to generate the implementation details.
